pageHero.heading: 10 Essential Sun Protection Tips for Every Season
pageHero.subtext: Protecting your skin from UV damage is crucial year-round, not just in summer.

article.title: 10 Essential Sun Protection Tips for Every Season
article.excerpt: Learn how to protect your skin from harmful UV rays throughout the year with these expert-recommended strategies from our dermatologists.
article.content:
Sun protection is one of the most important steps in any skincare routine, yet many people only think about it during summer beach days. The truth is, UV rays can damage your skin year-round, even on cloudy days and through windows.

Here are 10 essential sun protection strategies recommended by our board-certified dermatologists:

1. Wear Sunscreen Daily

Apply broad-spectrum SPF 30 or higher every morning, regardless of the weather or season. UV rays penetrate clouds and can reflect off snow, water, and even concrete.

2. Reapply Throughout the Day

Sunscreen effectiveness decreases over time. Reapply every two hours when outdoors, and immediately after swimming or sweating.

3. Don't Forget Often-Missed Areas

Ears, neck, hands, and the tops of feet are frequently neglected but highly susceptible to sun damage and skin cancer.

4. Seek Shade During Peak Hours

UV rays are strongest between 10 AM and 4 PM. When possible, plan outdoor activities outside these hours.

5. Wear Protective Clothing

Long sleeves, wide-brimmed hats, and UV-blocking sunglasses provide excellent physical sun protection.

6. Check Your Medications

Some medications increase sun sensitivity. Ask your dermatologist if any of your prescriptions require extra sun protection.

7. Use Window Film

Consider UV-blocking film for car and home windows, especially if you spend significant time near windows.

8. Remember Winter Protection

Snow reflects up to 80% of UV rays, doubling your exposure during winter sports and activities.

9. Protect Your Lips

Use SPF lip balm daily. Lips are vulnerable to sun damage and skin cancer.

10. Perform Regular Skin Checks

Examine your skin monthly for new or changing moles. Early detection is key to treating skin cancer successfully.

By following these guidelines consistently, you'll significantly reduce your risk of premature aging, sunburn, and skin cancer. If you have questions about sun protection for your specific skin type, schedule a consultation with our dermatology team.
article.author: Dr. Jennifer Roberts
